 36→public class KtLintRuleEngine(
 37→    /**
 38→     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39→     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40→     */
 41→    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42→    /**
 43→     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 44→     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45→     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46→     */
 47→    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48→    /**
 49→     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50→     * property being set in any other way.
 51→     */
 52→    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53→    /**
 54→     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55→     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56→     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57→     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58→     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59→     */
 60→    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61→    /**
 62→     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63→     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64→     */
 65→    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66→    /**
 67→     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68→     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69→     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70→     * is included.
 71→     */
 72→    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73→) {
 74→    init {
 75→        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 76→            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81→
 82→    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83→        EditorConfigLoader(
 84→            fileSystem,
 85→            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86→            editorConfigDefaults,
 87→            editorConfigOverride,
 88→        )
 89→
 90→    /**
 91→     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92→     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93→     *
 94→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96→     */
 97→    public fun lint(
 98→        code: Code,
 99→        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100→    ) {
101→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102→
103→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105→
106→        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107→            .visitor()
108→            .invoke { rule ->
109→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111→                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112→                }
113→            }
114→
115→        errors
116→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118→
119→        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120→    }
121→
122→    /**
123→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125→     *
126→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
127→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
128→     */
129→    public fun format(
130→        code: Code,
131→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
132→    ): String {
133→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
134→
135→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
136→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
137→
138→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
139→        var formatRunCount = 0
140→        var mutated: Boolean
141→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
142→        do {
143→            mutated = false
144→            formatRunCount++
145→            visitorProvider
146→                .visitor()
147→                .invoke { rule ->
148→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
149→                        if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
150→                            mutated = true
151→                            /**
152→                             * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
153→                             * suppression hints might have changed.
154→                             */
155→                            ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
156→                        }
157→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
158→                        errors.add(
159→                            Pair(
160→                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
161→                                // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
162→                                // does correct the error.
163→                                canBeAutoCorrected,
164→                            ),
165→                        )
166→                        // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be different
167→                        // from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it cn be helpful to know the exact order in which
168→                        // violations are being solved.
169→                        LOGGER.trace {
170→                            "Format violation: ${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
171→                                canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
172→                        }
173→                    }
174→                }
175→        } while (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE)
176→        if (formatRunCount == MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE && mutated) {
177→            LOGGER.warn {
178→                "Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file " +
179→                    "${code.filePathOrStdin()} in $MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE consecutive runs of format."
180→            }
181→        }
182→
183→        errors
184→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
185→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
186→
187→        if (!mutated && formatRunCount == 1) {
188→            return code.content
189→        }
190→
191→        val formattedCode =
192→            ruleExecutionContext
193→                .rootNode
194→                .text
195→                .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
196→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
197→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
198→        } else {
199→            formattedCode
200→        }.also {
201→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
206→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
207→        return when {
208→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
209→            else -> "\n"
210→        }
211→    }
212→
213→    /**
214→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
215→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
216→     *
217→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
218→     * ```properties
219→     * final-newline=true
220→     * indent-size=4
221→     * ```
222→     */
223→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
224→        val codeStyle =
225→            editorConfigOverride
226→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
227→                ?.parsed
228→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
229→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
230→        val rules =
231→            ruleProviders
232→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
233→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
234→                .toSet()
235→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
236→            .generateEditorconfig(
237→                rules,
238→                codeStyle,
239→                filePath,
240→            )
241→    }
242→
243→    /**
244→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
245→     */
246→    public fun trimMemory() {
247→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
248→    }
249→
250→    /**
251→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
252→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
253→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
254→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
255→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
256→     */
257→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
258→
259→    /**
260→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
261→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
262→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
263→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
264→     */
265→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
266→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
267→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
268→        )
269→    }
270→
271→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
272→
273→    public companion object {
274→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
275→
276→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
277→
278→        private const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
279→    }
280→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Gets the rule provider for the [KtLintRuleEngine] by applying the [ruleFilters] in the given order on the set of [RuleProvider]s provided
 8→ * by the previous (or the initial list of [RuleProvider]s).
 9→ */
10→internal fun KtLintRuleEngine.ruleProviders(vararg ruleFilters: RuleFilter): Set<RuleProvider> {
11→    var ruleProviders = initialRuleProviders()
12→    val ruleFilterIterator = ruleFilters.iterator()
13→    while (ruleFilterIterator.hasNext()) {
14→        val ruleFilter = ruleFilterIterator.next()
15→        ruleProviders = ruleFilter.filter(ruleProviders)
16→    }
17→    return ruleProviders
18→}
19→
20→private fun KtLintRuleEngine.initialRuleProviders() =
21→    ruleProviders
22→        .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
23→        .toSet()
24→
25→internal interface RuleFilter {
26→    fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider>
27→}

 29→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 30→    val code: Code,
 31→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 32→    val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
 33→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 34→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 35→) {
 36→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 37→
 38→    init {
 39→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 43→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode, editorConfig)
 44→    }
 45→
 46→    fun executeRule(
 47→        rule: Rule,
 48→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 49→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 50→    ) {
 51→        try {
 52→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 53→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 54→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 55→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 56→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 57→                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
 58→                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
 59→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),
 60→            )
 61→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→            rule.afterLastNode()
 63→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 64→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 65→                e.line,
 66→                e.col,
 67→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 68→                """
 69→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 70→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 71→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 72→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 73→                """.trimIndent(),
 74→                e.cause,
 75→            )
 76→        }
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 80→        node: ASTNode,
 81→        rule: Rule,
 82→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        /**
 86→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 87→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 88→         */
 89→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 90→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 91→            try {
 92→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 93→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 94→                }
 95→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 96→                    node
 97→                        .getChildren(null)
 98→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 99→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
100→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
101→                                    childNode,
102→                                    rule,
103→                                    autoCorrect,
104→                                    emit,
105→                                )
106→                            }
107→                        }
108→                }
109→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
110→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
111→                }
112→            } catch (e: Exception) {
113→                if (autoCorrect) {
114→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
115→                    // original AST
116→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
117→                        rule,
118→                        0,
119→                        0,
120→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
121→                        e.cause ?: e,
122→                    )
123→                } else {
124→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
125→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
126→                        rule,
127→                        line,
128→                        col,
129→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
130→                        e.cause ?: e,
131→                    )
132→                }
133→            }
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    companion object {
138→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
139→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
140→            code: Code,
141→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
142→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine)
143→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
144→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
145→
146→            val psiFileName =
147→                code
148→                    .filePath
149→                    ?.pathString
150→                    ?: if (code.script) {
151→                        "File.kts"
152→                    } else {
153→                        "File.kt"
154→                    }
155→            val psiFile =
156→                psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
157→                    psiFileName,
158→                    KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
159→                    normalizedText,
160→                ) as KtFile
161→            psiFile
162→                .findErrorElement()
163→                ?.let { errorElement ->
164→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
165→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
166→                }
167→
168→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
169→
170→            val editorConfig =
171→                ktLintRuleEngine
172→                    .editorConfigLoader
173→                    .load(code.filePath)
174→                    .also {

183→                    .ruleProviders(

190→                rootNode,
191→                ruleProviders,
192→                editorConfig,
193→                positionInTextLocator,
194→            )
195→        }

197→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
198→            return text
199→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
200→                .replace("\r", "\n")
201→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
202→        }

204→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
205→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
206→                return this
207→            }
208→            this.children.forEach { child ->
209→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
210→                if (errorElement != null) {
211→                    return errorElement
212→                }
213→            }
214→            return null
215→        }
216→    }

220→    propertyName: String,
221→    ktlintVersion: String,
222→) {
223→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
224→        LOGGER.warn {
225→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
226→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
227→        }
228→    }
229→}

 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 4→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 5→
 6→internal class SuppressHandler(
 7→    private val suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator,
 8→    private val autoCorrect: Boolean,
 9→    private val emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
10→) {
11→    fun handle(
12→        node: ASTNode,
13→        ruleId: RuleId,
14→        function: (Boolean, (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit) -> Unit,
15→    ) {
16→        val suppress =
17→            suppressionLocator(
18→                node.startOffset,
19→                ruleId,
20→            )
21→        val autoCorrect = this.autoCorrect && !suppress
22→        val emit =
23→            if (suppress) {
24→                SUPPRESS_EMIT
25→            } else {
26→                this.emit
27→            }
28→        function(autoCorrect, emit)
29→    }
30→
31→    private companion object {
32→        // Swallow violation so that it is not emitted
33→        val SUPPRESS_EMIT: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _, _ -> }
34→    }
35→}

 9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
20→
21→/**
22→ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23→ */
24→public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25→    StandardRule(
26→        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28→            setOf(
29→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31→            ),
32→        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33→    ),
34→    Rule.Experimental,
35→    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37→        node: ASTNode,
38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40→    ) {
41→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42→            val afterBlockComment =
43→                node
44→                    .leaves()
45→                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46→                    .firstOrNull()
47→                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48→
49→            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50→                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51→                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52→            ) {
53→                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54→                if (autoCorrect) {
55→                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56→                }
57→            }
58→        }
59→    }
60→
61→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65→        rawRemove()
66→    }
67→
68→    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69→
70→    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71→    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72→        this
73→            ?.text
74→            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75→            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76→            ?.trim()
77→            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78→            ?: false
79→}
80→
81→public val NO_SINGLE_LINE_BLOCK_COMMENT_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule().ruleId

 44→/**
 45→ * The [Rule] contains the life cycle hooks which are called by the KtLint rule engine to execute the rule.
 46→ *
 47→ * The implementation of a [Rule] **doesn't** have to be thread-safe or stateless provided that the [RuleProvider] creates a new instance of
 48→ * [Rule] on each call to [RuleProvider.provider].
 49→ *
 50→ * When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the [ruleId] and [about] fields, so that it is
 51→ * clear to users whenever they used the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint
 52→ * project.
 53→ */
 54→public open class Rule(
 55→    /**
 56→     * Identification of the rule. A [ruleId] has a value that must adhere the convention "<rule-set-id>:<rule-id>". The rule set id
 57→     * 'standard' is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API
 58→     * Consumers should use a prefix other than 'standard' to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
 59→     */
 60→    public open val ruleId: RuleId,

 62→     * About the rule. Background information about the rule and its maintainer. About information is meant to be used in stack traces or
 63→     * API consumers to provide more detailed information about the rule.

 68→     * independent of all other rules.
 69→     */

 71→    /**
 72→     * Set of [EditorConfigProperty]'s that are to provided to the rule. Only specify the properties that are actually used by the rule.
 73→     */
 74→    public open val usesEditorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = emptySet(),
 75→) {
 76→    private var traversalState = TraversalState.NOT_STARTED
 77→
 78→    /**
 79→     * This method is called once before the first node is visited. It can be used to initialize the state of the rule
 80→     * before processing of nodes starts.
 81→     */
 82→    public open fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {}
 83→
 84→    /**
 85→     * This method is called on a node in AST before visiting the child nodes. This is repeated recursively for the
 86→     * child nodes resulting in a depth first traversal of the AST.
 87→     *
 88→     * @param node AST node
 89→     * @param autoCorrect indicates whether rule should attempt autocorrection
 90→     * @param emit a way for rule to notify about a violation (lint error)
 91→     */
 92→    public open fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 96→    ) {}
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * This method is called on a node in AST after all its child nodes have been visited.
100→     */
101→    @Suppress("unused")
102→    public open fun afterVisitChildNodes(
103→        node: ASTNode,
104→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
105→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
106→    ) {}
107→
108→    /**
109→     * This method is called once after the last node in the AST is visited. It can be used for teardown of the state
110→     * of the rule.
111→     */
112→    public open fun afterLastNode() {}
113→
114→    /**
115→     * Checks whether the [Rule] instance is used for traversal of the AST and as of that potentially has changed the state of the [Rule]
116→     * provided that it has state.
117→     */
118→    public fun isUsedForTraversalOfAST(): Boolean = traversalState != TraversalState.NOT_STARTED
119→
120→    /**
121→     * Marks the [Rule] instance as being used for traversal of an AST. From this moment on, this instance of the [Rule]
122→     * can not be used to start a new traversal of the same or another AST as the instance might contain state.
123→     */
124→    public fun startTraversalOfAST() {
125→        require(traversalState == TraversalState.NOT_STARTED)
126→        traversalState = TraversalState.CONTINUE
127→    }
128→
129→    /**
130→     * Checks whether the next node in the AST is to be traversed. By default, the entire AST is traversed.
131→     */
132→    public fun shouldContinueTraversalOfAST(): Boolean = traversalState == TraversalState.CONTINUE
133→
134→    /**
135→     * Stops traversal of the AST. Intended usage it to prevent parsing of the remainder of the AST once the goal of the
136→     * rule is achieved. For example, if the ".editorconfig" property indent_size is set to 0 or -1 then the indent rule
137→     * should be disabled.
138→     *
139→     * When called in [beforeFirstNode], no AST nodes will be visited. [afterLastNode] is still called.
140→     *
141→     * When called in [beforeVisitChildNodes], the child nodes of that node will not be visited. [afterVisitChildNodes]
142→     * is still called for the node and each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally
143→     * [afterLastNode] is called.
144→     *
145→     * When called in [afterVisitChildNodes] the child nodes of that node are already visited. [afterVisitChildNodes] is
146→     * still called for each of its parent nodes. Other nodes in the AST will not be visited. Finally [afterLastNode] is
147→     * called.
148→     *

150→     */

153→    }
154→
155→    private enum class TraversalState {
156→        /**
157→         * Traversal of the AST is not started. As no life cycle hooks of the [Rule] have been executed, the [Rule]
158→         * instance can not contain state specific for the AST.
159→         */
160→        NOT_STARTED,
161→
162→        /**
163→         * Traversal of the AST is started and should be continued with next node.
164→         */
165→        CONTINUE,
166→
167→        /**
168→         * Stops traversal of yet unvisited nodes in the AST. See [stopTraversalOfAST] for more details.
169→         */
170→        STOP,
171→    }
172→
173→    /**
174→     * About the rule. Background information about the rule and its maintainer. About information is meant to be used in stack traces or
175→     * API consumers to provide more detailed information about the rule. Please provide all details below, so that users of your rule set
176→     * can easily get up-to-date information about the rule.
177→     */
178→    public data class About(
179→        /**
180→         * Name of person, organisation or group maintaining the rule.
181→         */
182→        val maintainer: String = "Not specified (and not maintained by the Ktlint project)",
183→        /**
184→         * Url to the repository containing the rule.
185→         */
186→        val repositoryUrl: String = "Not specified",
187→        /**
188→         * Url to the issue tracker of the project which provides the rule.
189→         */
190→        val issueTrackerUrl: String = "Not specified",
191→    )
192→
193→    public sealed class VisitorModifier {
194→        /**
195→         * Defines that the [Rule] that declares this [VisitorModifier] will be run after the [Rule] with rule id
196→         * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId].
197→         */
198→        public data class RunAfterRule(
199→            /**
200→             * The [RuleId] of the [Rule] which should run before the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
201→             */
202→            val ruleId: RuleId,
203→            /**
204→             * The [Mode] determines whether the [Rule] that declares this [VisitorModifier] can be run in case the [Rule] with rule id
205→             * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is not loaded or enabled.
206→             */
207→            val mode: Mode,
208→        ) : VisitorModifier() {
209→            public enum class Mode {
210→                /**
211→                 * Run the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule] regardless whether the [Rule] with ruleId
212→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is loaded or disabled. However, if that other rule is loaded and enabled, it runs
213→                 * before the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
214→                 */
215→                REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
216→
217→                /**
218→                 * Run the [Rule] that declares the [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule] only in case the [Rule] with ruleId
219→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.ruleId] is loaded *and* enabled. That other rule runs before the [Rule] that declares the
220→                 * [VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule].
221→                 */
222→                ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
223→            }
224→        }

226→        public object RunAsLateAsPossible : VisitorModifier()
227→    }
228→
229→    /**
230→     * This interface marks a rule as an 'experimental' rule. A rule marked with this interface will only be executed by ktlint in case the

236→    /**

241→    public interface OfficialCodeStyle

 7→internal data class FormatterTags(
 8→    val formatterTagOff: String?,
 9→    val formatterTagOn: String?,
10→) {
11→    companion object {
12→        private val DISABLED_FORMATTER_TAGS = FormatterTags(null, null)
13→
14→        fun from(editorConfig: EditorConfig): FormatterTags =
15→            if (editorConfig[FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY]) {
16→                FormatterTags(
17→                    formatterTagOff = editorConfig[FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY],
18→                    formatterTagOn = editorConfig[FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY],
19→                )
20→            } else {
21→                DISABLED_FORMATTER_TAGS
22→            }
23→
24→        val FORMATTER_TAGS_ENABLED_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
25→            EditorConfigProperty(
26→                type =
27→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
28→                        "ij_formatter_tags_enabled",
29→                        "When enabled, IntelliJ IDEA Formatter tags will be respected (e.g. disable and enable all ktlint rules for the " +
30→                            "code enclosed between the formatter tags.",
31→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
32→                        setOf(true.toString(), false.toString()),
33→                    ),
34→                defaultValue = false,
35→            )
36→
37→        val FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<String> =
38→            EditorConfigProperty(
39→                type =
40→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
41→                        "ij_formatter_off_tag",
42→                        "The IntelliJ IDEA formatter tag to disable formatting. This also disables the ktlint rules.",
43→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
44→                    ),
45→                defaultValue = "@formatter:off",
46→            )
47→
48→        val FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<String> =
49→            EditorConfigProperty(
50→                type =
51→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
52→                        "ij_formatter_on_tag",
53→                        "The IntelliJ IDEA formatter tag to enable formatting. This also enables the ktlint rules.",
54→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
55→                    ),
56→                defaultValue = "@formatter:on",
57→            )
58→    }
59→}

332→    private class NoFooIdentifierRule(id: RuleId) : Rule(
333→        ruleId = id,
334→        about = About(),
335→    ) {
336→        override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
337→            node: ASTNode,
338→            autoCorrect: Boolean,
339→            emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
340→        ) {
341→            if (node.elementType == ElementType.IDENTIFIER && node.text.startsWith("foo")) {
342→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
343→            }
344→        }
345→    }
346→
347→    private fun lint(
348→        code: String,
349→        editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
350→    ) = ArrayList<LintError>().apply {
351→        KtLintRuleEngine(
352→            ruleProviders =
353→                setOf(
354→                    // The same rule is supplied once a standard rule and once as non-standard rule. Note that the
355→                    // ruleIds are different.
356→                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
357→                    RuleProvider { NoFooIdentifierRule(NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID) },
358→                ),
359→            editorConfigOverride =
360→                editorConfigOverride
361→                    .plus(
362→                        STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
363→                        NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.enabled,
364→                    ),
365→        ).lint(Code.fromSnippet(code)) { e -> add(e) }
366→    }
367→
368→    private fun lintError(
369→        line: Int,
370→        col: Int,
371→        ruleId: String,
372→    ) = LintError(line, col, RuleId(ruleId), "Line should not contain a foo identifier", false)
373→
374→    private companion object {
375→        val NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID = "custom".also { require(it != RuleSetId.STANDARD.value) }
376→
377→        val STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("standard:no-foo-identifier-standard")
378→        val NON_STANDARD_NO_FOO_IDENTIFIER_RULE_ID = RuleId("$NON_STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:no-foo-identifier")
379→    }
380→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.RuleExecution
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName
 15→
 16→/**
 17→ * Filters the [RuleProvider]s defined in the [KtLintRuleEngine] for [Rule]s which are enabled for the given [EditorConfig].
 18→ */
 19→internal class RuleExecutionRuleFilter(
 20→    private val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 21→) : RuleFilter {
 22→    override fun filter(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Set<RuleProvider> {
 23→        val ruleExecutionFilter =
 24→            RuleExecutionFilter(
 25→                editorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders),
 26→                editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 27→            )
 28→        return ruleProviders
 29→            .filter { ruleExecutionFilter.isEnabled(it) }
 30→            .toSet()
 31→    }
 32→
 33→    private fun EditorConfig.ruleExecutionProperties(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): Map<String, RuleExecution> {
 34→        val ruleExecutionPropertyNames =
 35→            ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders)
 36→                .plus(ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders))
 37→                .plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name)
 38→        return map { it.name to RULE_EXECUTION_PROPERTY_TYPE.parse(it.sourceValue).parsed }
 39→            .toMap()
 40→            .filterValues { it != null }
 41→            .filterKeys { it in ruleExecutionPropertyNames }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    private fun ruleExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 45→        ruleProviders
 46→            .map { it.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName() }
 47→            .distinct()
 48→
 49→    private fun ruleSetExecutionPropertyNames(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>) =
 50→        ruleProviders
 51→            .map { it.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName() }
 52→            .distinct()
 53→}
 54→
 55→/**
 56→ * Creates a filter of [RuleExecution] properties. This filter is basically an extract of the [EditorConfig] which allows to retrieve rule
 57→ * execution properties for which no proper [EditorConfigProperty] is defined as rules have no need to be able to extract such a property
 58→ * from [EditorConfig].
 59→ */
 60→private class RuleExecutionFilter(
 61→    val ruleExecutionProperties: Map<String, RuleExecution>,
 62→    val codeStyleValue: CodeStyleValue,
 63→) {
 64→    fun isEnabled(ruleProvider: RuleProvider) = isRuleEnabled(ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance())
 65→
 66→    private fun isRuleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 67→        /*
 68→         * If set for the rule, the rule execution property takes precedence above other checks. This allows for execution of a specific
 69→         * experimental or ktlint_official code style rule without enabling them all. Also, this allows to disable a specific rule in case
 70→         * the experimental and/or ktlint_official code style rules are enabled.
 71→         */
 72→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName())
 73→            ?.let { it == RuleExecution.enabled }
 74→            ?: isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule)
 75→
 76→    private fun isRuleConditionallyEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 77→        when {
 78→            rule is Rule.Experimental && rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 79→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule) && isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 80→            rule is Rule.Experimental ->
 81→                isExperimentalEnabled(rule)
 82→            rule is Rule.OfficialCodeStyle ->
 83→                isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule)
 84→            else ->
 85→                isRuleSetEnabled(rule)
 86→        }
 87→
 88→    private fun isExperimentalEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 89→        ruleExecution(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) == RuleExecution.enabled &&
 90→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
 91→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
 92→
 93→    private fun isOfficialCodeStyleEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 94→        codeStyleValue == CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official &&
 95→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled &&
 96→            ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ktLintRuleExecutionPropertyName()) != RuleExecution.disabled
 97→
 98→    private fun isRuleSetEnabled(rule: Rule) =
 99→        ruleExecution(rule.ruleId.ruleSetId.ktLintRuleSetExecutionPropertyName())
100→            .let { ruleSetExecution ->
101→                if (ruleSetExecution?.name == EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY.name) {
102→                    // Rules in the experimental rule set are only run when enabled explicitly.
103→                    ruleSetExecution == RuleExecution.enabled
104→                } else {
105→                    // Rules in other rule sets are enabled by default.
106→                    ruleSetExecution != RuleExecution.disabled
107→                }
108→            }
109→
110→    private fun ruleExecution(ruleExecutionPropertyName: String) = ruleExecutionProperties[ruleExecutionPropertyName]
111→}

 22→/**
 23→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered.
 24→ */
 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(
 27→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 28→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 29→            setOf(
 30→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 32→            ),
 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val beforeBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .leaves(false)
 43→                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
 44→                    .firstOrNull()
 45→                    ?: node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 46→            val afterBlockComment =
 47→                node
 48→                    .leaves()
 49→                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
 50→                    .firstOrNull()
 51→                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 52→
 53→            if (!beforeBlockComment.prevLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() &&
 54→                !afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
 55→            ) {
 56→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(beforeBlockComment, afterBlockComment)) {
 57→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 58→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 59→                    //    some comment
 60→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 61→                    emit(
 62→                        node.startOffset,
 63→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 64→                            "disallowed",
 65→                        false,
 66→                    )
 67→                } else if (beforeBlockComment.prevLeaf()?.elementType == LBRACE &&
 68→                    afterBlockComment.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RBRACE
 69→                ) {
 70→                    // Allow single line blocks containing a block comment
 71→                    //   val foo = { /* no-op */ }
 72→                    return
 73→                } else {
 74→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 75→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 76→                    emit(node.startOffset, "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed", false)
 77→                }
 78→                return
 79→            }
 80→
 81→            beforeBlockComment
 82→                .prevLeaf()
 83→                .takeIf { !it.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() }
 84→                ?.let {
 85→                    if (node.textContains('\n')) {
 86→                        // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 87→                        emit(
 88→                            node.startOffset,
 89→                            "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
 90→                            false,
 91→                        )
 92→                    } else {
 93→                        emit(
 94→                            node.startOffset,
 95→                            "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 96→                            true,
 97→                        )
 98→                        if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
100→                        }
101→                    }
102→                }
103→
104→            afterBlockComment
105→                .nextLeaf()
106→                .takeIf { !it.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() }
107→                ?.let { nextLeaf ->
108→                    emit(
109→                        nextLeaf.startOffset,
110→                        "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line",
111→                        true,
112→                    )
113→                    if (autoCorrect) {
114→                        nextLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
115→                    }
116→                }
117→        }
118→    }
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
121→}
122→
123→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId

 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,

 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**

 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)

125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()

282→    private fun code() =

293→    public companion object {
294→        /**
295→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
296→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
297→         */
298→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
299→
300→        /**
301→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
302→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
303→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
304→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
305→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
306→         */
307→
308→        public fun assertThatRule(
309→            provider: () -> Rule,
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
313→
314→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
315→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
316→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
317→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
318→            { code ->
319→                KtLintAssertThat(
320→                    ruleProvider = this,
321→                    code = code,
322→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
323→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
324→                )
325→            }
326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }

355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
417→     */
418→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()
424→
425→        return this
426→    }
427→
428→    /**
429→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
430→     *
431→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
432→     * formatted.
433→     *
434→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
435→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
436→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
437→     * violated.
438→     */
439→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
440→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
441→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
442→        }
443→
444→        val lintErrors = lint()
445→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
446→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
447→
448→        return this
449→    }
450→
451→    /**
452→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
453→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
454→     */
455→    public fun hasLintViolation(
456→        line: Int,
457→        col: Int,
458→        detail: String,
459→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
460→        hasLintViolations(
461→            LintViolation(
462→                line = line,
463→                col = col,
464→                detail = detail,
465→            ),
466→        )
467→
468→    /**
469→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
470→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
471→     */
472→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
473→        line: Int,
474→        col: Int,
475→        detail: String,
476→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
477→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
478→            LintViolation(
479→                line = line,
480→                col = col,
481→                detail = detail,
482→            ),
483→        )
484→
485→    /**
486→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
487→     */
488→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
489→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
490→
491→        val actualLintViolationFields =
492→            lint()
493→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
494→                .toLintViolationsFields()
495→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
496→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
497→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
498→        return this
499→    }
500→
501→    /**
502→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
503→     * automatically corrected.
504→     */
505→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
506→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
507→
508→        val actualLintViolationFields =
509→            lint()
510→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
511→                .toLintViolationsFields()
512→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
513→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
514→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
515→        return this
516→    }
517→
518→    /**
519→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
520→     */
521→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
522→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
523→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
524→        }
525→
526→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
527→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
528→
529→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
530→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
531→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
532→
533→        return this
534→    }
535→
536→    /**
537→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
538→     */
539→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
540→        line: Int,
541→        col: Int,
542→        detail: String,
543→    ): Unit =
544→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
545→            LintViolation(
546→                line = line,
547→                col = col,
548→                detail = detail,
549→            ),
550→        )
551→
552→    /**
553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
554→     * corrected.
555→     */
556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
557→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
558→
559→        val actualLintViolationFields =
560→            lint()
561→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
562→                .map {
563→                    LintViolationFields(
564→                        line = it.line,
565→                        col = it.col,
566→                        detail = it.detail,
567→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
568→                    )
569→                }.toTypedArray()
570→        val expectedLintViolationFields =
571→            expectedLintViolations
572→                .map {
573→                    LintViolationFields(
574→                        line = it.line,
575→                        col = it.col,
576→                        detail = it.detail,
577→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
578→                    )
579→                }
580→
581→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
582→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
583→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolationFields.toTypedArray())
584→    }

586→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> {
587→        return map {
588→            LintViolationFields(
589→                line = it.line,
590→                col = it.col,
591→                detail = it.detail,
592→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
593→            )
594→        }.toTypedArray()
595→    }

597→    private fun List<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId }
598→
599→    private fun List<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId }
600→
601→    private fun List<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
602→        map {
603→            LintViolationFields(
604→                line = it.line,
605→                col = it.col,
606→                detail = it.detail,
607→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
608→            )
609→        }.toTypedArray()

611→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
612→        val ruleProviders =
613→            setOf(ruleProvider)
614→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
615→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
616→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
617→        val editorConfigOverride =
618→            editorConfigOverride
619→                .enableExperimentalRules()
620→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
621→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
622→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
623→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
624→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
625→        )
626→    }
627→
628→    private fun lint(): List<LintError> {
629→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
630→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
631→        return lintErrors
632→    }
633→
634→    private fun format(): Pair<String, List<LintError>> {
635→        val lintErrors = ArrayList<LintError>()
636→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
637→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
638→    }
639→
640→    /* Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made
641→     * against the original [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
642→     */
643→    private data class LintViolationFields(
644→        val line: Int,
645→        val col: Int,
646→        val detail: String,
647→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
648→    )
649→}

651→internal class MissingEolMarker : RuntimeException(
652→    """
653→    The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
654→    constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
655→    '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
656→    """.trimIndent(),
657→)

659→/**
660→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be
661→ * derived from the rule for which the AssertThat was created.
662→ */
663→public data class LintViolation(
664→    val line: Int,
665→    val col: Int,
666→    val detail: String,
667→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
668→)
669→
670→/**
671→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already
672→ * provided.
673→ */
674→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
675→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
676→        ruleProviders
677→            .asSequence()
678→            .map { ruleProvider ->
679→                ruleProvider
680→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
681→                    .ruleId
682→                    .ruleSetId
683→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
684→            }.distinct()
685→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
686→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
687→            .toList()
688→            .toTypedArray()
689→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
690→}
691→
692→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
693→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)

 12→import mu.KotlinLogging
 13→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 14→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
